generatedSentence: It was the dawning of a new era, but the old tavern still buzzed with a lively hum, its viburnum decorations wilting on the backside of the bar. My grandson, a lean youth with a stomach rumbling from sparse rations, watched the dwarfs playing cards with a limp lamprey dealer. An advert for woolly alien rugs flickered on a screen above the grimy walkups, a stark vignette of this struggling district. He was ready to do what was necessary, his spirit not mellow, despite the levying taxes the mayor had imposed. He had learnt to live with minimal leeway, unlike the vain rockers who frequented the place. His honour was all he had left. A stranger with a satanic grin and a serpent tattoo appeared, her underdress shimmering with chroma. She didn't scold the chicks she passed, but her presence caused a roundup of uneasy glances. My grandson felt a familiar reactive feeling that something was lurking beneath the city's greenway – an underside of corruption he'd only heard about in hushed tones from the junkmen. He knew he must keep up his practice, for he felt betrayed by the system, like a gopher caught in a trap. He was wanting to find the source of the effluent that poisoned the causeway and the mines, an unfair situation created by gamblers and warriors. The plot thickened; a knacker, a samurai, and even some relict vikings were involved in a bounty on him, bluffed by uploaded numbers from a paywall, a brilliant adjoint to their scheme. The stranger, with her jacquard cloak, started juggling data, making their uploads slower, but the dynamos and clockwork mechanisms of the foe's finance network were too powerful. Our hero’s stomach buckled at the thought of the havocs unleashed, a fear as primal as cannibalism. He had no choice but to beseech the listening kookaburra, which held the secret code, hoping its call wasn't just a vain trick. His feelings were raw, knowing this was his one uncut shot at redemption; he could not fall down now. In the brilliant neon galaxies, a final frappe of combat ensued; he keyed into the system, offering a vaccine against their digital plague, much like a vaccinia shot. He admitted his fear but stood firm, his samurai spirit strong, ideally leading the charge against the stingy operators who had caused this urinary blight. The stingrays of light from the broken servers illuminated the chaos as he managed to score a critical hit, causing the entire frame to fall. Laters, he ensured all records of the satanic system were packed and erased. The festivals of freedom would soon begin, a brilliant reward for his defensive, yet ultimately victorious, stand.
panelSentences: It was the dawning of a new era, but the old tavern still buzzed with a lively hum, its viburnum decorations wilting on the backside of the bar. My grandson, a lean youth with a stomach rumbling from sparse rations, watched the dwarfs playing cards with a limp lamprey dealer. An advert for woolly alien rugs flickered on a screen above the grimy walkups, a stark vignette of this struggling district. He was ready to do what was necessary, his spirit not mellow, despite the levying taxes the mayor had imposed. He had learnt to live with minimal leeway, unlike the vain rockers who frequented the place. His honour was all he had left.

A stranger with a satanic grin and a serpent tattoo appeared, her underdress shimmering with chroma. She didn't scold the chicks she passed, but her presence caused a roundup of uneasy glances. My grandson felt a familiar reactive feeling that something was lurking beneath the city's greenway – an underside of corruption he'd only heard about in hushed tones from the junkmen. He knew he must keep up his practice, for he felt betrayed by the system, like a gopher caught in a trap. He was wanting to find the source of the effluent that poisoned the causeway and the mines, an unfair situation created by gamblers and warriors.

The plot thickened; a knacker, a samurai, and even some relict vikings were involved in a bounty on him, bluffed by uploaded numbers from a paywall, a brilliant adjoint to their scheme. The stranger, with her jacquard cloak, started juggling data, making their uploads slower, but the dynamos and clockwork mechanisms of the foe's finance network were too powerful. Our hero’s stomach buckled at the thought of the havocs unleashed, a fear as primal as cannibalism. He had no choice but to beseech the listening kookaburra, which held the secret code, hoping its call wasn't just a vain trick. His feelings were raw, knowing this was his one uncut shot at redemption; he could not fall down now.

In the brilliant neon galaxies, a final frappe of combat ensued; he keyed into the system, offering a vaccine against their digital plague, much like a vaccinia shot. He admitted his fear but stood firm, his samurai spirit strong, ideally leading the charge against the stingy operators who had caused this urinary blight. The stingrays of light from the broken servers illuminated the chaos as he managed to score a critical hit, causing the entire frame to fall. Laters, he ensured all records of the satanic system were packed and erased. The festivals of freedom would soon begin, a brilliant reward for his defensive, yet ultimately victorious, stand.
rawPanelSentences: It was the dawning of a new era, but the old tavern still buzzed with a lively hum, its viburnum decorations wilting on the backside of the bar. My grandson, a lean youth with a stomach rumbling from sparse rations, watched the dwarfs playing cards with a limp lamprey dealer. An advert for woolly alien rugs flickered on a screen above the grimy walkups, a stark vignette of this struggling district. He was ready to do what was necessary, his spirit not mellow, despite the levying taxes the mayor had imposed. He had learnt to live with minimal leeway, unlike the vain rockers who frequented the place. His honour was all he had left.

A stranger with a satanic grin and a serpent tattoo appeared, her underdress shimmering with chroma. She didn't scold the chicks she passed, but her presence caused a roundup of uneasy glances. My grandson felt a familiar reactive feeling that something was lurking beneath the city's greenway – an underside of corruption he'd only heard about in hushed tones from the junkmen. He knew he must keep up his practice, for he felt betrayed by the system, like a gopher caught in a trap. He was wanting to find the source of the effluent that poisoned the causeway and the mines, an unfair situation created by gamblers and warriors.

The plot thickened; a knacker, a samurai, and even some relict vikings were involved in a bounty on him, bluffed by uploaded numbers from a paywall, a brilliant adjoint to their scheme. The stranger, with her jacquard cloak, started juggling data, making their uploads slower, but the dynamos and clockwork mechanisms of the foe's finance network were too powerful. Our hero’s stomach buckled at the thought of the havocs unleashed, a fear as primal as cannibalism. He had no choice but to beseech the listening kookaburra, which held the secret code, hoping its call wasn't just a vain trick. His feelings were raw, knowing this was his one uncut shot at redemption; he could not fall down now.

In the brilliant neon galaxies, a final frappe of combat ensued; he keyed into the system, offering a vaccine against their digital plague, much like a vaccinia shot. He admitted his fear but stood firm, his samurai spirit strong, ideally leading the charge against the stingy operators who had caused this urinary blight. The stingrays of light from the broken servers illuminated the chaos as he managed to score a critical hit, causing the entire frame to fall. Laters, he ensured all records of the satanic system were packed and erased. The festivals of freedom would soon begin, a brilliant reward for his defensive, yet ultimately victorious, stand.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, cyberpunk comic art, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist is a lean young man with a glowing blue cybernetic arm and a tattered, dark-green trench coat. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist in a dimly lit futuristic tavern, observing a game of cards, extreme wide shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist confronting a mysterious, cloaked stranger on a polluted neon-lit greenway, medium shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist frantically working at a holographic console, surrounded by menacing shadowy figures, dramatic low angle. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist standing victorious amidst shattered futuristic technology and bright data streams, cinematic full shot. vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting.

definitions.definition: To begin to brighten with daylight.
definitions.example: A new day dawns.

definitions.definition: To start to appear or be realized.
definitions.example: I don’t want to be there when the truth dawns on him.

definitions.definition: To begin to give promise; to begin to appear or to expand.
Dawn.
The first beginnings of something.
A building containing a bar licensed to sell alcoholic drinks, and usually offering accommodation; an inn.
Term of address.
Full of life; energetic.
Bright, glowing, vivid; strong, vigorous.
Endowed with or manifesting life; living.
Representing life; lifelike.
Airy; animated; spirited.
(of beer) Fizzy; foamy; tending to produce a large head in the glass.
Any of many shrubs and trees, of the genus Viburnum, native to the Northern Hemisphere that have showy clusters of flowers
The back side of anything, the part opposite its front, particularly:
The reverse or opposite of anything.
Any plant in the genus Saxifraga.
An organ in animals that stores food in the process of digestion.
The belly.
Pride, haughtiness.
Appetite.
definitions.example: a good stomach for roast beef

definitions.definition: Desire, appetite (for something abstract).
definitions.example: I have no stomach for a fight today.

definitions.definition: To tolerate (something), emotionally, physically, or mentally; to stand or handle something.
definitions.example: I can't stomach her cooking.

definitions.definition: To be angry.
To resent; to remember with anger; to dislike.
To turn the stomach of; to sicken or repel.
A son of one's child.

Any member of a race of beings from (especially Scandinavian and other Germanic) folklore, usually depicted as having some sort of supernatural powers and being skilled in crafting and metalworking, often as short with long beards, and sometimes as clashing with elves.
A person of short stature, often one whose limbs are disproportionately small in relation to the body as compared with normal adults, usually as the result of a genetic condition.
An animal, plant or other thing much smaller than the usual of its sort.
definitions.example: dwarf honeysuckle

definitions.definition: A star of relatively small size.
To render (much) smaller, turn into a dwarf (version).
To make appear (much) smaller, puny, tiny.
definitions.example: The newly-built skyscraper dwarfs all older buildings in the downtown skyline.

definitions.definition: To make appear insignificant.
definitions.example: Bach dwarfs all other composers.

definitions.definition: To become (much) smaller.
To hinder from growing to the natural size; to make or keep small; to stunt.
An irregular, jerky or awkward gait.
definitions.example: She walks with a limp.

definitions.definition: A scraper for removing poor ore or refuse from the sieve.
To walk lamely, as if favouring one leg.
(of a vehicle) To travel with a malfunctioning system of propulsion.
definitions.example: The bomber limped home on one engine.

definitions.definition: To move or proceed irregularly.
definitions.example: The business limped through the recession

definitions.definition: To call, particularly in an unraised pot pre-flop.
Any long slender primitive eel-like freshwater and saltwater fish of the order Petromyzontiformes, having a sucking mouth with rasping teeth but no jaw.
A sweater or similar garment made of wool.
A sheep not yet shorn.
A piece of woolwork.
Made of wool.
definitions.example: Put on a woolly jumper and turn down the thermostat.

definitions.definition: Having a thick, soft texture, as if made of wool.
definitions.example: There was nothing left in the fruit bowl but a brown banana and a couple of woolly pears.

definitions.definition: (of thinking, principles, etc.) Based on emotions rather than logic.
definitions.example: That's the sort of woolly thinking that causes wars to start.

definitions.definition: Unclear, fuzzy, hazy, cloudy.
Clothed in wool.
An apartment or block with stairs rather than an elevator.
An informal visit to a control tower by a pilot, typically used as part of pilot training.
A mountain that can be climbed without specialist equipment.
A customer who arrives without a reservation; a walk-in.
An indoor or outdoor stand where food is sold to the public.
Colaptes auratus, northern flicker.
An advertisement, an ad.
Any of several species of kingfishers in the genus Dacelo, known for their laugh-like call.
Of, pertaining to or resembling Satan (the Devil).
definitions.example: Luciferians and Laveyans follow a Satanic religion.

definitions.definition: Evil, fiendish, devilish or diabolical.
Of or pertaining to any form of Satanism.
A snake.
An obsolete wind instrument in the brass family, whose shape is suggestive of a snake (Wikipedia article).
A subtle, treacherous, malicious person.
A kind of firework with a serpentine motion.
To wind or meander
To encircle.
To burn with hot liquid.
definitions.example: to scald the hand

definitions.definition: To heat almost to boiling.
definitions.example: Scald the milk until little bubbles form.

definitions.definition: A person who habitually scolds, in particular a troublesome and angry woman.
To rebuke angrily.
A curved piece of wood attached to the bottom of a rocking chair or cradle that enables it to rock back and forth.
A rocking chair.
The lengthwise curvature of a surfboard. (More rocker is a more curved board.)
definitions.example: All modern surfboards share a similar rocker design — Bruce Jones https//web.archive.org/web/20051212041317/http://www.brucejones.com/longboar.htm

definitions.definition: The breve below as in ḫ.
Someone passionate about rock music.
A musician who plays rock music.
A rock music song.
One who rocks something.
A member of a British subculture of the 1960s, opposed to the mods, who dressed in black leather and were interested in 1950s music.
Any implement or machine working with a rocking motion, such as a trough mounted on rockers for separating gold dust from gravel, etc., by agitation in water.
A tool with small teeth that roughens a metal plate to produce tonality in mezzotints.
A rocking horse.
A rocker board.
A skate with a curved blade, somewhat resembling in shape the rocker of a cradle.
A rock shaft.

Any of several large herbivorous pachyderms native to Africa and Asia of the five extant species in the three extant genera in the family Rhinocerotidae, with thick, gray skin and one or two horns on their snouts.
Ready money; cash
To prepare; to make ready for action.
Prepared for immediate action or use.

definitions.definition: Inclined; apt to happen.
Liable at any moment.
definitions.example: The seed is ready to sprout.

definitions.definition: Not slow or hesitating; quick in action or perception of any kind.

definitions.definition: Offering itself at once; at hand; opportune; convenient.
The drift of a ship or airplane in a leeward direction.
A varying degree or amount of freedom or flexibility; margin, latitude, elbowroom.

definitions.definition: An adverse discrepancy or variation in a cumulative process, usually in the phrase make up leeway.
To acquire, or attempt to acquire knowledge or an ability to do something.

definitions.definition: To come to know; to become informed of; to find out.

definitions.definition: To teach.

definitions.definition: Overly proud of oneself, especially concerning appearance; having a high opinion of one's own accomplishments with slight reason.

definitions.definition: Showy; ostentatious.

definitions.definition: The structural elements of a building or other constructed object.

definitions.definition: Anything composed of parts fitted and united together; a fabric; a structure.

definitions.definition: A piece of photographic film containing an image.

An individually scrollable region of a webpage.
An inning.

A stage or level of a video game.

A form of knowledge representation in artificial intelligence.
A complete lattice in which meets distribute over arbitrary joins.
To fit, as for a specific end or purpose; make suitable or comfortable; adapt; adjust.
To construct by fitting or uniting together various parts; fabricate by union of constituent parts.

To profit; avail.
To fit; accord.

Backward; slow.

Not belonging to one.

definitions.definition: An outsider or foreigner.
One not admitted to communion or fellowship.
A newcomer.

One not privy or party to an act, contract, or title; a mere intruder or intermeddler; one who interferes without right.
definitions.example: Actual possession of land gives a good title against a stranger having no title.

To estrange; to alienate.
Repetition of an activity to improve a skill.

A customary action, habit, or behaviour; a manner or routine.

definitions.definition: Actual operation or experiment, in contrast to theory.

definitions.definition: The form, manner, and order of conducting and carrying on suits and prosecutions through their various stages, according to the principles of law and the rules laid down by the courts.
definitions.example: This firm of solicitors is involved in family law practice.

definitions.definition: Skilful or artful management; dexterity in contrivance or the use of means; stratagem; artifice.
A easy and concise method of applying the rules of arithmetic to questions which occur in trade and business.
To repeat (an activity) as a way of improving one's skill in that activity.

definitions.definition: To pursue (a career, especially law, fine art or medicine).

To sprout, as seed does in the ground; to vegetate.

The colorfulness relative to the brightness of a similarly illuminated area
The aspect of a colour's hue that depends on the amount of white or black in it; saturation
A note in a chromatic scale
A relaxed mood.
To make mellow; to relax or soften.

Soft or tender by reason of ripeness; having a tender pulp.

definitions.definition: Easily worked or penetrated; not hard or rigid.

definitions.definition: Not coarse, rough, or harsh; subdued, soft, rich, delicate; said of sound, color, flavor, style, etc.
Well matured; softened by years; genial; jovial.
Relaxed; calm; easygoing; laid-back.
Warmed by liquor, slightly intoxicated, stoned, or high.
My; belonging to me; that which belongs to me.
Something that, or someone who, survives or remains or is left over after the loss of others; a relic.
Surviving, remaining.
That is a relict; pertaining to a relict.
To remain concealed in order to ambush.
To remain unobserved.
To hang out or wait around a location, preferably without drawing attention to oneself.

To saddle (a person) with an undesirable task or duty.
The act of one who lurks.
A seller of junk.
To wish for or desire (something); to feel a need or desire for; to crave or demand.
definitions.example: What do you want to eat?  I want you to leave.  I never wanted to go back to live with my mother.

definitions.definition: (in particular) To wish, desire or demand to see, have the presence of or do business with.
definitions.example: Danish police want him for embezzlement.

definitions.definition: To desire (to experience desire); to wish.
definitions.example: You can leave if you want.

definitions.definition: (usually second person, often future tense) To be advised to do something (compare should, ought).
definitions.example: You’ll want to repeat this three or four times to get the best result.

definitions.definition: (now colloquial) To lack and be in need of or require (something, such as a noun or verbal noun).
definitions.example: That chair wants fixing.

definitions.definition: (now rare) To have occasion for (something requisite or useful); to require or need.
To be lacking or deficient or absent.
definitions.example: There was something wanting in the play.

definitions.definition: To be in a state of destitution; to be needy; to lack.
definitions.example: The paupers desperately want.

definitions.definition: To lack and be without, to not have (something).
definitions.example: She wanted anything she needed.

definitions.definition: (by extension) To lack and (be able to) do without.
That wants or desires.
Absent or lacking.
Deficient.
Without, except, but.
Less, short of, minus.
The transpose of the cofactor matrix of a given square matrix.
(of a matrix) Transpose conjugate.
(of an operator) Hermitian conjugate.
A functor related to another functor by an adjunction.

A curve A such that any point of a given curve C of multiplicity r has multiplicity at least r–1 on A. Sometimes the multiple points of C are required to be ordinary, and if this condition is not satisfied the term sub-adjoint is used.
An assistant to someone who holds a position in the military or civil service.
An assistant mayor of a French commune.
Used in certain contexts, in each case involving a pair of transformations, one of which is, or is analogous to, conjugation (either inner automorphism or complex conjugation).
(of a functor) That is related to another functor by an adjunction.
(of one curve to another curve) Having a relationship of the nature of an adjoint (adjoint curve); sharing multiple points with.
To distort or collapse under physical pressure; especially, of a slender structure in compression.
To make bend; to cause to become distorted.
To give in; to react suddenly or adversely to stress or pressure (of a person).

definitions.definition: To yield; to give way; to cease opposing.
To enter upon some labour or contest; to join in close fight; to contend.
To buckle down; to apply oneself.
To fasten using a buckle.
To unite in marriage.
A finely cut gemstone, especially a diamond, cut in a particular form with numerous facets so as to maximize light return through the top (called "table") of the stone.
The size of type between excelsior and diamond, standardized as 4-point.
Most hummingbird species of the genus Heliodoxa.
A kind of cotton goods, figured on the weaving.
Shining brightly.
definitions.example: the brilliant lights along the promenade

definitions.definition: (of a colour) Both bright and saturated.
definitions.example: butterflies with brilliant blue wings

definitions.definition: (of a voice or sound) Having a sharp, clear tone
Of surpassing excellence.
definitions.example: The actor's performance in the play was simply brilliant.

definitions.definition: Magnificent or wonderful.
Highly intelligent.
definitions.example: She is a brilliant scientist.

definitions.definition: A proposal that has been made.
definitions.example: I decline your offer to contract.

definitions.definition: Something put forth, bid, proffered or tendered.
definitions.example: His offer was $3.50 per share.

definitions.definition: An invitation to enter into a binding contract communicated to another party which contains terms sufficiently definite to create an enforceable contract if the other party accepts the invitation.
definitions.example: His first letter was not a real offer, but an attempt to determine interest.

definitions.definition: To fit (a lock) with a key.
To fit (pieces of a mechanical assembly) with a key to maintain the orientation between them.
To mark or indicate with a symbol indicating membership in a class.
(telegraphy and radio telegraphy) To depress (a telegraph key).
To operate (the transmitter switch of a two-way radio).
(more usually to key in) To enter (information) by typing on a keyboard or keypad.
definitions.example: Our instructor told us to key in our user IDs.

definitions.definition: To vandalize (a car, etc.) by scratching with an implement such as a key.
definitions.example: He keyed the car that had taken his parking spot.

definitions.definition: To link (as one might do with a key or legend).
To be identified as a certain taxon when using a key.
To modify (an advertisement) so as to target a particular group or demographic.
To attune to; to set at; to pitch.
To fasten or secure firmly; to fasten or tighten with keys or wedges.
An abstract entity used to describe quantity.
definitions.example: Zero, one, −1, 2.5, and pi are all numbers.

definitions.definition: A numeral: a symbol for a non-negative integer.
definitions.example: The number 8 is usually made with a single stroke.

definitions.definition: An element of one of several sets: natural numbers, integers, rational numbers, real numbers, complex numbers, and sometimes extensions such as hypercomplex numbers, etc.
definitions.example: The equation e^{i\pi}+1=0 includes the most important numbers: 1, 0, \pi, i, and e.

definitions.definition: (Followed by a numeral; used attributively) Indicating the position of something in a list or sequence. Abbreviations: No or No., no or no. (in each case, sometimes written with a superscript "o", like Nº or №). The symbol "#" is also used in this manner.
definitions.example: Horse number 5 won the race.

definitions.definition: Quantity.
definitions.example: Any number of people can be reading from a given repository at a time.

definitions.definition: A sequence of digits and letters used to register people, automobiles, and various other items.
definitions.example: Her passport number is C01X864TN.

definitions.definition: A telephone number.
(grammar) Of a word or phrase, the state of being singular, dual or plural, shown by inflection.
definitions.example: Adjectives and nouns should agree in gender, number, and case.

definitions.definition: (in the plural) Poetic metres; verses, rhymes.
A performance; especially, a single song or song and dance routine within a larger show.
definitions.example: For his second number, he sang "The Moon Shines Bright".

definitions.definition: A person.
An item of clothing, particularly a stylish one.
A marijuana cigarette, or joint; also, a quantity of marijuana bought form a dealer.
An issue of a periodical publication.
definitions.example: the latest number of a magazine

definitions.definition: A large amount, in contrast to a smaller amount; numerical preponderance.
To label (items) with numbers; to assign numbers to (items).
definitions.example: Number the baskets so that we can find them easily.

definitions.definition: To total or count; to amount to.
definitions.example: I don’t know how many books are in the library, but they must number in the thousands.

definitions.definition: Many individuals as a group.
definitions.example: Numbers of people have visited the fair.

definitions.definition: A small burrowing rodent, especially in the family Geomyidae.
The gopher tortoise.
The gopher rockfish.
An electricity generator, a dynamo-electric machine.
An energetic person.
definitions.example: That new Vice President of Marketing is a real dynamo; sales have already gone up this quarter.

definitions.definition: A means, attitude or position of defense.
Intended for defence; protective.
definitions.example: a defensive perimeter

definitions.definition: Intended to deter attack.
definitions.example: a defensive missile system

definitions.definition: Performed so as to minimise risk.
definitions.example: defensive driving

definitions.definition: Displaying an inordinate sensitivity to criticism or intrusion.
Of a bowling or fielding tactic designed to prevent the other side from scoring runs; of a batting tactic designed to prevent being out.
Pertaining to defense, as opposed to attack.
definitions.example: The victim held up his hand to repel the attacker and received a defensive knife wound.

definitions.definition: In a state or posture of defense.
The chief executive of the municipal government of a city, borough, &c., formerly usually appointed as a caretaker by European royal courts but now usually appointed or elected locally.
Short for mayor of the palace, the royal stewards of the Frankish Empire.
Synonym of mair, various former officials in the Kingdom of Scotland.
A member of a city council.
A high justice, an important judge.
A largely ceremonial position in some municipal governments that presides over the city council while a contracted city manager holds actual executive power.
A local VIP, a muckamuck or big shot reckoned to lead some local group.
An activity in which cattle are herded together in order to be inspected, counted, branded or shipped.
(law enforcement) The similar police activity of gathering together suspects.
The forcible gathering together of any particular group of people.
The summary to a news bulletin.
An upward curvature or convexity, as in the deck of a vessel.
The finishing of an arrangement.
A portion of some limited resource allocated to a person or group.
To supply with a ration; to limit (someone) to a specific allowance of something.
definitions.example: We rationed ourselves to three sips of water a day until we were rescued.

definitions.definition: To portion out (especially during a shortage of supply); to limit access to.
definitions.example: By the third day on the raft, we had to ration our water.

definitions.definition: To restrict (an activity etc.)
definitions.example: Our present health care system is rationed only to those who can afford it because of unnecessary high cost, lack of insurance coverage by 47 million people, and exorbitant prescription prices.

definitions.definition: To impose (a tax or fine) to collect monies due, or to confiscate property.
definitions.example: to levy a tax

definitions.definition: To raise or collect by assessment; to exact by authority.
To draft someone into military service.
To raise; to collect; said of troops, to form into an army by enrollment, conscription. etc.
To wage war.
To raise, as a siege.
To erect, build, or set up; to make or construct; to raise or cast up.
definitions.example: to levy a mill, dike, ditch, a nuisance, etc.

definitions.definition: The act by which something is levied.
definitions.example: levyings of war

definitions.definition: A running ornament consisting of leaves and tendrils, used in Gothic architecture.
A decorative design, originally representing vine branches or tendrils, at the head of a chapter, of a manuscript or printed book, or in a similar position.
(by extension) Any small borderless picture in a book, especially an engraving, photograph, or the like, which vanishes gradually at the edge.
(by extension) A short story or anecdote that presents a scene or tableau, or paints a picture.
The central pictorial image on a postage stamp.

The characteristic of a camera lens, either by deficiency in design or by mismatch of the lens with the film format, to produce an image smaller than the film's frame with a crudely focused border. Photographers may deliberately choose this characteristic for a special effect.
A small sticker affixed to a vehicle windscreen to indicate that tolls have been paid.
To make, as an engraving or a photograph, with a border or edge gradually fading away.
A clear distilled alcoholic liquor made from grain mash.
A serving of the above beverage.
Neutral spirits distilled (or treated after distillation) so as to have no distinctive character, aroma, taste or color.
To come or be in sight; to be in view; to become visible.
To come before the public.
definitions.example: A great writer appeared at that time.

definitions.definition: To stand in presence of some authority, tribunal, or superior person, to answer a charge, plead a cause, etc.; to present oneself as a party or advocate before a court, or as a person to be tried.
To become visible to the apprehension of the mind; to be known as a subject of observation or comprehension, or as a thing proved; to be obvious or manifest.
To seem; to have a certain semblance; to look.
definitions.example: He appeared quite happy with the result.

definitions.definition: To bring into view.
To dress in insufficiently warm clothes.
To dress in insufficiently formal clothes.
(heading) To be moved downwards.
To move downwards.
To happen, to change negatively.
To be allotted to; to arrive through chance, fate, or inheritance.
definitions.example: And so it falls to me to make this important decision.  The estate fell to his brother; the kingdom fell into the hands of his rivals.

definitions.definition: To diminish; to lessen or lower.
To bring forth.
definitions.example: to fall lambs

definitions.definition: To issue forth into life; to be brought forth; said of the young of certain animals.
To descend in character or reputation; to become degraded; to sink into vice, error, or sin.
To become ensnared or entrapped; to be worse off than before.
definitions.example: to fall into error;  to fall into difficulties

definitions.definition: To assume a look of shame or disappointment; to become or appear dejected; said of the face.
To happen; to come to pass; to chance or light (upon).
To begin with haste, ardour, or vehemence; to rush or hurry.
definitions.example: After arguing, they fell to blows.

definitions.definition: To be dropped or uttered carelessly.
definitions.example: An unguarded expression fell from his lips.

definitions.definition: (of a fabric) To hang down (under the influence of gravity).
definitions.example: An Empire-style dress has a high waistline – directly under the bust – from which the dress falls all the way to a hem as low as the floor.

definitions.definition: Present participle of fall
That falls or fall.
definitions.example: a falling leaf

definitions.definition: Unwilling to spend, give, or share; ungenerous; mean
Small, scant, meager, insufficient
The total number of goals, points, runs, etc. earned by a participant in a game.
definitions.example: The player with the highest score is the winner.

definitions.definition: The number of points accrued by each of the participants in a game, expressed as a ratio or a series of numbers.
definitions.example: The score is 8-1 even though it's not even half-time!

definitions.definition: The performance of an individual or group on an examination or test, expressed by a number, letter, or other symbol; a grade.
definitions.example: The test scores for this class were high.

definitions.definition: Twenty, 20 (number).
definitions.example: Some words have scores of meanings.

definitions.definition: A distance of twenty yards, in ancient archery and gunnery.
A weight of twenty pounds.
The written form of a musical composition showing all instrumental and vocal parts below each other.
The music of a movie or play.
Subject.
Account; reason; motive; sake; behalf.
A notch or incision; especially, one that is made as a tally mark; hence, a mark, or line, made for the purpose of account.
An account or reckoning; account of dues; bill; debt.
A criminal act, especially:
A sexual conquest.
To cut a notch or a groove in a surface.
definitions.example: The baker scored the cake so that the servers would know where to slice it.

definitions.definition: To record the tally of points for a game, a match, or an examination.
To obtain something desired.
To provide (a film, etc.) with a musical score.
Acknowledgement of success
A mechanism powered by a coiled spring and regulated by some form of escapement; the power is transmitted through toothed gearwheels and used to drive a mechanical clock, toy, or other device.
Recognition of importance or value; respect; veneration (of someone, usually for being morally upright or successful)
definitions.example: The crowds gave the returning general much honor and praise.

definitions.definition: The state of being morally upright, honest, noble, virtuous, and magnanimous; excellence of character; the perception of such a state; favourable reputation; dignity
definitions.example: He was a most perfect knight, for he had great honor and chivalry.

definitions.definition: A token of praise or respect; something that represents praiseworthiness or respect, such as a prize or award given by the state to a citizen
definitions.example: Audie Murphy received many honors, such as the Distinguished Service Cross.

definitions.definition: A privilege
definitions.example: I had the honour of dining with the ambassador.

definitions.definition: (in the plural) the privilege of going first
definitions.example: I'll let you have the honours, Bob—go ahead.

definitions.definition: A cause of respect and fame; a glory; an excellency; an ornament.
definitions.example: He is an honour to his nation.

definitions.definition: (feudal law) a seigniory or lordship held of the king, on which other lordships and manors depended
The center point of the upper half of an armorial escutcheon (compare honour point)
In bridge, an ace, king, queen, jack, or ten especially of the trump suit. In some other games, an ace, king, queen or jack.
(in the plural) (courses for) an honours degree: a university qualification of the highest rank
definitions.example: At university I took honours in modern history.

definitions.definition: To think of highly, to respect highly; to show respect for; to recognise the importance or spiritual value of
definitions.example: The freedom fighters will be forever remembered and honored by the people.

definitions.definition: To conform to, abide by, act in accordance with (an agreement, treaty, promise, request, or the like)
definitions.example: I trusted you, but you have not honored your promise.

definitions.definition: To confer (bestow) an honour or privilege upon (someone)
definitions.example: Ten members of the profession were honored at the ceremony.

definitions.definition: To make payment in respect of (a cheque, banker's draft, etc.)
definitions.example: I'm sorry Sir, but the bank did not honour your cheque.

definitions.definition: One of the Scandinavian or other Northern European seafaring warriors that raided (and then settled) the British Isles and other parts of Europe in the 8th to the 11th centuries and, according to many historians, were the first Europeans to reach North America.
(by extension) A stock character common in the fantasy genre, namely a barbarian, generally equipped with an axe or sword and a helmet adorned with horns.
A Norseman (mediaeval Scandinavian).
(mildly) An ethnic Swede, Norwegian, Dane, Icelander or Faroe Islander.
A player on the Minnesota Vikings NFL team.
To fall to the ground. To collapse.
definitions.example: Ring a-ring o' roses, / A pocketful of posies. / A-tishoo! A-tishoo! / We all fall down. — traditional nursery rhyme (British version)

definitions.definition: To sail or drift toward the mouth of a river or other outlet.
To fail.
definitions.example: That is where your reasoning falls down.

definitions.definition: Any of various large, venomous rays, of the orders Rajiformes and Myliobatiformes, having a barbed, whiplike tail.
A device that simulates a cell tower, used to intercept cell phone communications.
Taking a long time to move or go a short distance, or to perform an action; not quick in motion; proceeding at a low speed.
definitions.example: a slow train;  a slow computer

definitions.definition: Not happening in a short time; spread over a comparatively long time.
Of reduced intellectual capacity; not quick to comprehend.
Not hasty; not tending to hurry; acting with deliberation or caution.
(of a clock or the like) Behind in time; indicating a time earlier than the true time.
definitions.example: That clock is slow.

definitions.definition: Lacking spirit; deficient in liveliness or briskness.
(of a period of time) Not busy; lacking activity.
definitions.example: I'm just sitting here with a desk of cards, enjoying a slow afternoon.

definitions.definition: Slowly.
definitions.example: That clock is running slow.

definitions.definition: See you later; an expression used at parting.
Not cut.
(of a gemstone) Not cut or ground to the desired shape.
(of a book) Not having the page edges trimmed.
(of a play, film etc) Not edited or abridged by a censor.
Not circumcised.
To transfer data to a computer on a network, especially to a server on the Internet.
definitions.example: You will have to convert your photograph to a jpg format if you wish to upload it.

definitions.definition: The process by which something is uploaded.
A urinary device or fixture: a urinal.
Pertaining to urine, its production, function, or excretion.
Of or relating to the organs involved in the formation and excretion of urine.
Liqueur poured over shaved ice.
A thick milkshake containing ice cream.
(Greece) An iced, sweetened, beaten coffee drink.
(physical) To put or bring things together in a limited or confined space, especially for storage or transport.
(social) To cheat.
To load with a pack
definitions.example: to pack a horse

definitions.definition: To load; to encumber.
To move, send or carry.
To block a shot, especially in basketball.
(of the forwards in a rugby team) To play together cohesively, specially with reference to their technique in the scrum.
(of a drag king, transman, etc.) To wear a prosthetic penis inside one’s trousers for better verisimilitude.
Put into a package.
definitions.example: packed lunch

definitions.definition: Filled with a large number or large quantity of something.
definitions.example: packed with goodness

definitions.definition: Filled to capacity with people.
definitions.example: The bus was packed and I couldn't get on.

definitions.definition: Generosity; also an act of generosity.
Something given liberally; a gift.
A reward for some specific act, especially one given by an authority or a government.
An abundance or wealth.
To make a bluff; to give the impression that one's hand is stronger than it is.
definitions.example: John bluffed by betting without even a pair.

definitions.definition: (by analogy) To frighten or deter with a false show of strength or confidence; to give a false impression of strength or temerity in order to intimidate and gain some advantage.
definitions.example: The government claims it will call an election if this bill does not pass. Is it truly ready to do so, or is it bluffing?

definitions.definition: To take advantage by bluffing.
definitions.example: We bluffed our way past the guards.

definitions.definition: To fluff, puff or swell up.
Such a file transfer.
To transfer data to a computer on a network, especially to a server on the Internet.
definitions.example: You will have to convert your photograph to a jpg format if you wish to upload it.

definitions.definition: In feudal Japan, a soldier who served a daimyo.
Someone who listens, especially to a speech or a broadcast.
(chiefly Java) A function that runs in response to an event; an event handler.
A person's ear.
An infection of cowpox.
(by extension) The virus which causes this infection.
To deliver into the hands of an enemy by treachery or fraud, in violation of trust; to give up treacherously or faithlessly.
definitions.example: an officer betrayed the city

definitions.definition: To prove faithless or treacherous to, as to a trust or one who trusts; to be false to; to deceive.
definitions.example: My eyes have been betraying me since I turned sixty.

definitions.definition: To violate the confidence of, by disclosing a secret, or that which one is bound in honor not to make known.
To disclose or indicate, for example something which prudence would conceal; to reveal unintentionally.
definitions.example: Though he had lived in England for many years, a faint accent betrayed his Swedish origin.

definitions.definition: To mislead; to expose to inconvenience not foreseen; to lead into error or sin.
To lead astray; to seduce (as under promise of marriage) and then abandon.
One who makes knickknacks, toys, etc.
One of two or more pieces of bone or wood held loosely between the fingers, and struck together by moving the hand; a clapper.
A harness maker.
One who slaughters and (especially) renders worn-out livestock (especially horses) and sells their flesh, bones and hides.
One who dismantles old ships, houses, etc. and sells their components.
A member of the Travelling Community; a Gypsy.
(Northern English) A person of lower social class; a chav, skanger or scobe.
(chiefly in the plural) A testicle.
A collier's horse.
(UK slang) To tire out, exhaust.
definitions.example: Carrying that giant statue up those stairs knackered me out

definitions.definition: (UK slang) To reprimand.
definitions.example: Digital giants Dstv and Vision Group’s Bukedde Television didn’t go untouched with the former lashed for laxities in re-connection especially in cases where a subscriber renewed their subscription by Mobile Money, while the latter got knackered for promoting witchcraft and witch doctors. ( http//trumpetnews.co.ug/2017/03/16/1615/ )

definitions.definition: Sensation, particularly through the skin.
definitions.example: The wool on my arm produced a strange feeling.

definitions.definition: Emotion; impression.
definitions.example: The house gave me a feeling of dread.

definitions.definition: (always in the plural) Emotional state or well-being.
definitions.example: You really hurt my feelings when you said that.

definitions.definition: (always in the plural) Emotional attraction or desire.
definitions.example: Many people still have feelings for their first love.

definitions.definition: Intuition.
definitions.example: He has no feeling for what he can say to somebody in such a fragile emotional condition.

definitions.definition: An opinion, an attitude.
The management of money and other assets.
The science of management of money and other assets.
(usually in the plural) Monetary resources, especially those of a public entity or a company.
definitions.example: Who's really in charge of a democracy's finances?

definitions.definition: The provision of a loan, payment instalment terms, or similar arrangement, to enable a customer to purchase an item without paying the full amount straight away.
definitions.example: Finance on all our new cars is provided by ABC Loans Ltd.

definitions.definition: To conduct, or procure money for, financial operations; manage finances.
To pay ransom.
To manage financially; be financier for; provide or obtain funding for a transaction or undertaking.
definitions.example: He financed his home purchase through a local credit union.

definitions.definition: To extort ransom from.
The Milky Way; the apparent band of concentrated stars which appears in the night sky over earth.
Any of the collections of many millions or billions of stars, galactic dust, black holes, etc. existing as independent and coherent systems, of which there are billions in the known universe.
Any print or pattern reminiscent of a galaxy, generally consisting of blending, semiopaque patches of vibrant color on a dark background.

A road that is raised, so as to be above water, marshland, and similar low-lying obstacles. Originally causeways were much like dykes, generally pierced to let water through, whereas many modern causeways are more like bridges or viaducts.
To pave, to cobble.
The act of eating another of one's own species.
An act in which one thing consumes or takes over another of the same kind.
In speech, the occurrence of one word eliding part or all of the next word, because the syllables are the same. For example, "Look, an MIT shirt" for "Look, an MIT T-shirt".
A percussive musical instrument spanned with a thin covering on at least one end for striking, forming an acoustic chamber, affecting what materials are used to make it; a membranophone.
Any similar hollow, cylindrical object.
definitions.example: Replace the drum unit of your printer.

definitions.definition: A barrel or large cylindrical container for liquid transport and storage.
definitions.example: The restaurant ordered ketchup in 50-gallon drums.

definitions.definition: The encircling wall that supports a dome or cupola.
Any of the cylindrical blocks that make up the shaft of a pillar.
A drumfish (family Sciaenidae).
A tip; a piece of information.
A small hill or ridge of hills.
A social gathering or assembly held in the evening.
A person's home; a house or other building, especially when insalubrious; a tavern, a brothel.
A small shallow drum.
A circular frame for embroidery.
A rich kind of gold and silver embroidery.
Silk or other material embroidered on a tambour.
The capital of a Corinthian column.
A work usually in the form of a redan, to enclose a space before a door or staircase, or at the gorge of a larger work. It is arranged like a stockade.

A shallow metallic cup or drum, with a thin elastic membrane supporting a writing lever. Two or more of these are connected by a rubber tube and used to transmit and register the movements of the pulse or of any pulsating artery.
In real tennis, a buttress-like obstruction in the main wall.
To embroider on a tambour (circular frame).
To pillage.
To cause havoc.
Fabric woven on a Jacquard loom.
definitions.example: The White House has jacquard drapes in the East Room.

definitions.definition: Fabric resembling a jacquard, but woven by a different process.
A Jacquard loom.
A type of sweet orange, normally seedless.
An impotent or infertile male.
A ball that is very difficult for the batsman to hit because it moves erratically either through the air or off the pitch
To allow to enter; to grant entrance (to), whether into a place, into the mind, or into consideration
definitions.example: A ticket admits one into a playhouse.

definitions.definition: To allow (someone) to enter a profession or to enjoy a privilege; to recognize as qualified for a franchise.
definitions.example: the prisoner was admitted to bail

definitions.definition: To concede as true; to acknowledge or assent to, as an allegation which it is impossible to deny
definitions.example: he admitted his guilt

definitions.definition: To be capable of; to permit. In this sense, "of" may be used after the verb, or may be omitted.
definitions.example: the words do not admit such a construction.

definitions.definition: To give warrant or allowance, to grant opportunity or permission (+ of).
definitions.example: circumstances do not admit of this

definitions.definition: To allow to enter a hospital or similar facility for treatment.
A request.
To beg or implore (a person)
To request or beg for
To make ugly
Not beautiful; uncomely; unattractive
Sorrowful; sad
Unseemly; disgraceful
Not fair, unjust
definitions.example: It was unfair for the boss to give larger bonuses to his friends.

definitions.definition: An event or community gathering, usually staged by a local community, which centers on some theme, sometimes on some unique aspect of the community.
In mythology, a set of celebrations in the honour of a god.
Fried cornbread
A corridor of undeveloped or park land.
One who plays at a game of chance, who gambles.
One who takes significant risks.
That reacts or responds to a stimulus
That readily takes part in reactions
definitions.example: francium is the most reactive of the alkali metals.

definitions.definition: Characterized by induction or capacitance rather than resistance.
Reacting to the past rather than anticipating the future, not predictive.
In an ideal way; perfectly.
Given ideal circumstances; preferably.
definitions.example: Ideally, we would have one textbook per student, but unfortunately some of you will have to share.

definitions.definition: To manipulate objects, such as balls, clubs, beanbags, rings, etc. in an artful or artistic manner. Juggling may also include assorted other circus skills such as the diabolo, devil sticks, hat, and cigar box manipulation as well.
definitions.example: She can juggle flaming torches.

definitions.definition: To handle or manage many tasks at once.
definitions.example: He juggled home, school, and work for two years.

definitions.definition: To deceive by trick or artifice.
To joke or jest.
To perform magic tricks.
A person who is actively engaged in battle, conflict or warfare; a soldier or combatant.
A person who is aggressively, courageously, or energetically involved in an activity, such as athletics.
